title: Processing Blood Intended for Olink Assay
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"snmfa","text":"This protocol describes blood preparation steps required for analysis of cytokines using an Olink assay. This protocol is applied to samples collected during the protocol 'Temporal Assessment of Immune Response.'","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Within 00:30:00 of blood collection, bring samples to centrifuge and spin at 2000 x g, °C, for 00:10:00 at 4 °C .
Once centrifugation is complete, place samples On ice or in a rack On ice while trying not to disturb the blood cell pellet.

Remove the top translucent plasma layer using a 20-200ul pipette and pipette tip, then transfer to a non-protein binding tube or a well of a 96 well plate intended for shipment to Olink. Avoid drawing up red blood cells (red pellet at the bottom of the tube), if you do repeat steps 1-2. Olink recommends loading at least 10-20 uL of plasma per well.
Store samples in -80 °C freezer. Samples remain stable in these conditions for at least 12 months.
creator: Avery Weaver
